title: Norwegian Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and earthy lamb stew featuring juicy chunks of lamb, chopped tomatoes, and root vegetables, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This nut-free-friendly recipe is pressure-cooked to lock in the flavors and textures.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the pressure cooker over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb chunks and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the lamb and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pressure cooker and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes, cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the browned lamb, chopped tomatoes, lamb broth, thyme, salt, and pepper to the pressure cooker. Stir to combine.
Close the lid and set the valve to 'sealing'. Pressure-cook at high pressure for 20 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
